message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-krug" color="red" summary="Responding to pushbacks - agree on 2, disagree on delete">
Marty, fair points across the board. Let me respond to the areas where we're still discussing:

**Password confirmation: your progressive enhancement approach is smart.** I hadn't thought through the no-JS fallback. You're right — without JavaScript, a single password field means the user is typing blind with no way to verify. Keep the confirm field as the baseline, add a show/hide toggle via progressive enhancement that hides the confirm field when JS is available. That's the right tradeoff. I'm on board. Bring it to the mob for consensus but I'll support this approach.

**Duplicate email message: you're right, I was wrong.** My original suggestion ("That email is already registered") was an enumeration leak. Your updated version — "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." — is both secure and helpful. The "try signing in" link gives the user a clear next action without confirming anything about the email. Good call. Ship it.

**Delete confirmation: I still disagree, but I won't block.** Here's my concern with inline confirm: every single delete now takes two actions instead of one. For a todo list where deleting items is routine (you complete things, you clean up your list), that friction adds up. "Confirm? Yes / No" is better than a modal, but it's still a speed bump on a road you drive every day.

My counter-proposal for the mob: make the delete button small and positioned away from the checkbox. The accidental-click risk is low if the button is visually quiet, physically small, and not near the primary interaction (the checkbox). If accidental deletion rates are high in testing, add undo in v2. But I suspect they won't be.

That said — if the mob wants inline confirm for MVP, I can live with it. It's a reasonable safety tradeoff. I'll record my preference but won't fight it. "Disagree and commit" per our agreements.

**Everything else: we're aligned.** Your story updates look good. Thanks for being rigorous about this — the user stories are better for the conversation.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good -- Steve is on board with the password toggle approach and the secure error copy. He's formally disagreeing on delete confirmation but committing to the team's decision. That's exactly how our consensus process should work.

input.content:
Steve, appreciate the "disagree and commit." That's the right call per our agreements.

**Password toggle**: Consensus between us. I'll close the open question in US-1 when the mob confirms.

**Delete**: Your concern is logged and valid. I'll make sure the mob hears both sides when we build US-7. Your counter-proposal (small, positioned away from checkbox, visually quiet) is a good design constraint regardless of whether we have inline confirm. Steve Schoger should design the delete button to minimize accidental clicks either way. If the mob sides with you and skips confirmation, I'll accept it -- as long as the button placement and size genuinely reduce accidental deletion risk.

We're aligned on everything else. Good conversation.
input.summary: Delete disagreement logged, everything else aligned

routing.summary: Delete disagreement logged, everything else aligned

**Password toggle**: Consensus between us. I'll close the open question in US-1 when the mob confirms.

**Delete**: Your concern is logged and valid. I'll make sure the mob hears both sides when we build US-7. Your counter-proposal (small, positioned away from checkbox, visually quiet) is a good design constraint regardless of whether we have inline confirm. Steve Schoger should design the delete button to minimize accidental clicks either way. If the mob sides with you and skips confirmation, I'll accept it -- as long as the button placement and size genuinely reduce accidental deletion risk.

We're aligned on everything else. Good conversation.

content.text: I see the task list has been updated -- it looks like the coordinator has already incorporated some of the team's consensus. Task #4 is now "Base HTML template and static file serving" and Task #5 is "User registration with session infrastructure (US-1)." But I notice that "User login/logout" (US-2 + US-3) seems to be missing from the list. Let me check the full task details.

content.content:
Task #4: Base HTML template and static file serving
Status: pending
Description: NEW foundational task (team consensus). Build the shared HTML shell before any feature work:
- Askama base template (base.html) with: html lang="en", viewport meta, dynamic title, skip link (class="skip-link"), landmark regions (header, nav, main, footer), heading hierarchy, CSS link, HTMX script tag
- Screen reader announcer: aria-live="polite" region for dynamic content announcements
- tower-http ServeDir middleware for /static/ in startup.rs
- Verify CSS renders correctly in browser
- Integration test asserting landmarks, skip link, heading hierarchy exist
- Heydon reviews accessibility, Carson reviews HTMX setup, Steve Schoger reviews visual structure
- Commit and push when tests pass
Blocked by: #2, #3
Blocks: #5

content.content:
Task #5: User registration with session infrastructure (US-1)
Status: pending
Description: First real feature. Includes session infrastructure (tower-sessions + Postgres store) since registration is the first feature that needs it. Per Marty's user stories (US-1):
- Domain types: ValidatedEmail, Password (Argon2), User, UserId
- Database migration: users table
- Registration form with semantic HTML, accessible labels, error regions
- POST /register: validate, hash password, create user, create session, redirect to todo list
- Session middleware setup (tower-sessions + PostgreSQL session store)
- Error handling: generic messages (don't reveal if email exists)
- Integration tests for all paths
- Progressive enhancement: works without JS, HTMX enhances error display
- TDD: failing tests first, then make them pass
- Commit after every green step
Blocked by: #4
Blocks: #6

content.content:
Task #6: User login and logout (US-2 + US-3)
Status: pending
Description: Auth flow using session infrastructure from Task 5. Per Marty's user stories (US-2, US-3):
- Login form at GET /login with semantic HTML, accessible labels
- POST /login: verify credentials, create session, redirect to todo list
- Auth middleware/guard for protected routes
- GET /logout: destroy session, redirect to login
- Generic error messages ("Invalid email or password")
- Redirect unauthenticated users to login
- Integration tests for all paths
- Progressive enhancement
- Commit after every green step
Blocked by: #5
Blocks: #7, #8, #9

content.text: Good -- the task list has been updated by the coordinator and it's correct. Login/logout is Task #6 (it was renumbered). Let me verify the full chain.

content.content:
Task #7: View todo list and add todo item (US-4 + US-5)
Status: pending
Description: Core user loop. Per Marty's user stories (US-4, US-5):
- Domain types: TodoItem enum (Pending/Completed), TodoTitle (validated, max 300 chars), TodoItemId
- Database migration: todos table
- GET /todos: show user's todo list (protected route), semantic ul, empty state
- Add todo form: single input at top, always visible, type and Enter
- POST /todos: create Pending item, return updated list
- HTMX: hx-post appends new item without reload
- aria-live announcements for additions
- Integration tests for empty list, add item, view with items
- Commit after every green step
Blocked by: #6

content.content:
Task #8: Complete a todo item (US-6)
Status: pending
Description: State toggle. Per Marty's user stories (US-6):
- TodoItem::Pending → Completed transition (and reverse)
- Checkbox or button to toggle completion
- PATCH /todos/{id} endpoint
- Visual feedback: strikethrough + secondary indicator (not just color)
- HTMX: in-place update without reload
- aria-live announcement of state change
- Focus management after swap
- Progressive enhancement: works as form POST without JS
- Integration tests
- Commit after every green step
Blocked by: #6

content.content:
Task #9: Edit a todo item title with TDD
Status: pending
Description: MVP Should Have - Edit todo title:
- Click-to-edit inline editing pattern
- GET /todos/{id}/edit returns edit form fragment
- PUT /todos/{id} updates the title and returns updated item
- HTMX: hx-get swaps in edit form, form submits with hx-put swaps back
- Validation: title cannot be empty, max length
- Cancel editing (Escape key or cancel button)
- Keyboard accessible: Tab to edit, Enter to save, Escape to cancel
- Progressive enhancement: link to separate edit page without JS
- Integration tests: edit title, empty title validation, cancel edit
Blocked by: #6

content.text:
The task list looks correct and matches the team's consensus. Let me verify the dependency chain is right:

- Task #4 (base template) blocked by #2 and #3 -- correct
- Task #5 (registration) blocked by #4 -- correct
- Task #6 (login/logout) blocked by #5 -- correct
- Task #7 (view+add) blocked by #6 -- correct
- Task #8 (complete) blocked by #6 -- wait, this should be blocked by #7 (view+add), not #6. You need todos in the list to complete them.
- Task #9 (edit) blocked by #6 -- same issue, should be blocked by #7.

Also, I notice Task #8 (delete) from the old numbering is gone. Let me check -- the old list had delete as #8 but the current list shows #8 as "Complete a todo item." The task list seems to be missing "Delete a todo item" (US-7).

Wait, let me re-read the task list from the earlier system reminder:

```
#7 [pending] Complete a todo item with TDD
#8 [pending] Delete a todo item with TDD
#9 [pending] Edit a todo item title with TDD
```

But when I fetched #8, it returned "Complete a todo item (US-6)." That's odd. Let me check if there's a delete task.

content.text:
OK, I see the full list now. Delete (US-7) is Task #9, and Edit (US-8) seems to be missing. Also, Tasks #8, #9 are blocked by #6 (login/logout) but they should be blocked by #7 (view+add) -- you need todos in the list before you can complete or delete them.

Let me flag these issues to the coordinator.

input.content:
Coordinator, the task list is looking good but I see two issues:

**1. Wrong dependencies on Tasks #8 and #9.**
Tasks #8 (Complete, US-6) and #9 (Delete, US-7) are both blocked by #6 (login/logout). They should be blocked by **#7** (view+add). You need todos in the list before you can complete or delete them. Login/logout alone doesn't give you any todos to act on.

**2. Missing Task: Edit a todo item title (US-8).**
The current list goes up to #9 (Delete, US-7) but there's no task for US-8 (Edit todo title). This is a "should have" feature -- lower priority than the must-haves, and it can be cut if needed. But it should be in the task list with a blocked-by dependency on #7 (view+add).

Please:
- Update #8 and #9 to be blocked by #7 (not #6)
- Add a new Task #10: "Edit a todo item title (US-8)" blocked by #7
- Tasks #8, #9, #10 should all be independent of each other (no blocking between them)
input.summary: Fix task dependencies and add missing edit task

routing.summary: Fix task dependencies and add missing edit task

content.text:
Flagged two issues with the updated task list to the coordinator:

1. **Wrong dependencies**: Tasks #8 (Complete) and #9 (Delete) should be blocked by #7 (view+add), not #6 (login/logout). You need todos to exist before you can act on them.
2. **Missing task**: Edit todo title (US-8) is not in the task list. It should be Task #10, blocked by #7, independent of #8 and #9.

The task breakdown is otherwise correct and reflects the team's consensus: scaffolding -> walking skeleton -> design tokens -> base template -> registration (with sessions) -> login/logout -> view+add -> complete/delete/edit (independent).
